text:
M Talking about it with the occupational physician:
= At the first pre-employment medical visit, everything depends
on the relational context and how the visit goes. || there is no legal obligation to talk about it but the
visible signs of the illness (low body weight, scars...)

can sometimes prompt questions! If needed, ask your primary care physician for a certificate stating the illness and indicating that it is being treated or even stabilized - to be used depending on the occupational physician’s reaction and their knowledge of the subject.
æ When returning to work, the occupational physician can intervene so the employer adapts the workstation. || they will declare the employee unfit for the position if there is too great a gap between the job’s requirements and the employee’s physical and psychological condition, which allows the search for another suitable position within the company.
